'''''Republic'' 62''' is the sixty-second issue in the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' series of comics. It was published on [[March 17]], [[2004]] by [[Dark Horse Comics]].

==Publisher's summary==
''Escaping from Asajj Ventress's fortress, Obi-Wan Kenobi and the clone trooper Alpha are pursued by Separatists and bounty hunters. Crash landing on a desolate planet, they are soon cornered. The bounty hunters give Alpha a choice: walk away from the fight, or die with his Jedi companion. Will Alpha's "live to fight another day" training win out? Or can the Force reveal another way to survive the ordeal before time runs out?''

==Plot summary==
{{Plot|comic}}

==Continuity==
''No Man's Land'' shows a [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] unmasked for the first time. However, this has not been proven to be their true appearance. It is possible that, because the image is shown in Anakin's dream, it is but a monstrous face that his own fears created.

When waking up from his nightmare, Anakin suddenly had two hands again. (Even though he lost his right under arm at the end of Attack of the Clones) In subsequent images this error does not occur. This could be a mistake by the artist or the colorist.

As the issue ends, [[Alpha (Advanced Recon Commando)|Alpha]] says goodbye to [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan]] before going to [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] to train the next batch of Clone Commanders. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin]] suggests that Alpha gives the trainees names instead of numbers, to which Alpha responds that he'll consider it. This may be the reason why all the commanders have names by the time of [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']].
